title: pittosporum × pourtetianum
intro: Pittosporum × pourtetianum, also known as the New Zealand laurel or Kohuhu, is a hybrid plant species in the family Pittosporaceae. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include the presence of alkaloids and flavonoids, and is traditionally used to treat various health issues. The plant has been found to have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, as well as promoting overall health and well-being. Herbal preparations of Pittosporum × pourtetianum can be made from the leaves and bark, which are often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal benefits.
